channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Tamilnadu Petroproducts Boosts LAB Capacity with Expanded Plant Startup
item.description: Tamilnadu Petroproducts Limited (TPL) announced its expanded Linear Alkyl Benzene (LAB) plant began operations on March 11, 2026. This expansion aims to significantly boost the company's production capacity in the petrochemical sector.

item.title: Mastercard Teams Up With Binance, PayPal for Blockchain Payments
item.description: Mastercard launched its Crypto Partner Program with over 85 digital asset and payments firms, including Binance, PayPal, and Ripple. These partners will work with Mastercard to connect blockchain technology to global payment systems, focusing on practical uses like cross-border transfers and business payments.

item.title: India's LPG Supply Crisis: Geopolitics, Misinformation Fuel Shortages
item.description: India's LPG supply chain is under pressure from Middle East geopolitical tensions, leading to shortages and price hikes. Misinformation is fueling panic bookings, while the government has ordered refineries to boost domestic production by 25% and prioritized household supplies. Despite these measures, the nation's heavy import reliance, with 60% sourced via sensitive shipping routes, highlights ongoing energy security vulnerabilities.

item.title: Canara Robeco AMC Appoints New Secretary, Updates Disclosure Code
item.description: Canara Robeco Asset Management Company has announced a change in its top compliance role and an update to its fair disclosure code. Ms. Hemangi Shailesh Patil will assume charge as Company Secretary and Compliance Officer on April 1, 2026, succeeding Mr. Ashutosh Pramod Vaidya. The company's Code of Practices and Procedures for Fair Disclosures has also been amended, effective from the same date, signalling a renewed focus on governance and transparency.

item.title: Middle East Tensions Trigger Market Sell-off, SIP Investors Find Opportunity
item.description: Middle East tensions sent Indian equity markets tumbling, with the BSE Sensex and Nifty 50 falling sharply. Higher crude prices fueled inflation worries. Despite market panic and a dip in stocks like Bajaj Finance, experts urge investors to continue Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs). Historically, these dips allow SIP investors to buy more units at lower prices, boosting future gains.

item.title: Binance Sues Wall Street Journal Over Report on Iran Sanctions Allegations
item.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Binance has filed a defamation lawsuit against Dow Jones & Company, publisher of The Wall Street Journal. The suit targets a WSJ report from February 23, 2026, which claimed U.S. officials were probing Binance for allegedly moving funds for Iran against American sanctions. Binance denies the report's claims as 'false and defamatory.' The company states that employees flagged for potential issues were fired for unauthorized data sharing, not for compliance concerns. This legal action comes as global regulatory oversight of crypto exchanges intensifies.

item.title: Brand Concepts Investor Meet: Public Updates on March 18, No New Data
item.description: Brand Concepts Limited will hold an investor and analyst meeting in Mumbai on March 18, 2026. The company has stated that discussions will be limited to publicly available information, with no price-sensitive data to be shared. The event aims to update stakeholders on operations and strategic initiatives.

item.title: Patel Logistics Eyes CSE Delisting; BSE, NSE Listing to Remain
item.description: Patel Integrated Logistics Limited will hold a board meeting on March 16, 2026, to consider a proposal for the voluntary delisting of its equity shares from the Calcutta Stock Exchange (CSE). The company intends to maintain its listing and trading status on the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and National Stock Exchange (NSE).

item.title: India's Corporate Debt: ₹140 Lakh Crore Need by FY31, ₹20 Lakh Crore Gap Looms
item.description: Corporate India is set to need ₹130–140 lakh crore in debt funding by FY31 to support major investments in infrastructure and industry, along with working capital and retail loan growth. Even with stronger balance sheets, a potential funding gap of ₹10–20 lakh crore is expected. Challenges include growing pressure on banks from high loan-to-deposit ratios, reliance on foreign borrowing sensitive to global rates, and the sheer volume of debt potentially straining markets.

item.title: Venmax Drugs Names Sadhanala as MD for 5 Years
item.description: Venmax Drugs & Pharmaceuticals has appointed Mr. Venkata Rao Sadhanala as its new Managing Director for a five-year term, effective March 11, 2026. He will receive an annual remuneration of ₹30 lakh, pending shareholder approval. Sadhanala, with over three decades of experience in banking, finance, and pharmaceuticals, was previously a Whole-time Director.

item.title: Saptak Chem Board to Name New MD, Review Loans March 17
item.description: Saptak Chem And Business Limited is holding a board meeting on March 17, 2026, to appoint Ayush Vinod Kumar Tated as Managing Director and review proposals for loans, investments, and related party transactions. Crucially, many of these decisions will require shareholder approval, marking a key moment for the company's strategy and leadership.

item.title: Gem Aromatics CFO Buys Shares, Signals Confidence in Growth
item.description: Gem Aromatics Limited announced its Whole-Time Director & CFO, Kaksha Vipul Parekh, bought 50,000 shares on March 11, 2026. This move raises his stake to 8.546% and is seen by many as a sign of confidence in the company's outlook.

item.title: Nilratan Suppliers Buys More Cropster Agro Shares, Boosting Stake to 5.79%
item.description: Nilratan Suppliers Private Limited has increased its shareholding in Cropster Agro Limited by acquiring 76,00,000 shares, or 0.90%, through an open market purchase on December 15, 2025. This transaction elevates Nilratan Suppliers' total stake to 5.79% from 4.89%, marking it as a more significant shareholder in the agro-trading company.

item.title: Sandhar Technologies: ICRA Withdraws ₹735 Crore Ratings at Company Request
item.description: Sandhar Technologies has asked ICRA to withdraw ratings on ₹150 crore term loans and ₹585 crore in working capital facilities, totaling ₹735 crore. India Ratings & Research's ratings remain active. This follows similar withdrawals by other agencies and shifts investor focus to transparency and financial health.

item.title: Canara Robeco AMC Names New Company Secretary, Revamps Disclosure Code
item.description: Canara Robeco Asset Management Company named Hemangi Shailesh Patil its new Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, starting April 1, 2026. This follows Mr. Ashutosh Pramod Vaidya's resignation. The company also updated its Fair Disclosure Code, effective the same date.

item.title: Tatva Chintan Pharma Chem to Meet Ask Wealth Advisor March 16
item.description: Tatva Chintan Pharma Chem Limited will hold a virtual meeting with Ask Wealth Advisor Private Limited on March 16, 2026. The company is engaging with the advisory firm to discuss its business and share information, which will later be posted on its website.

item.title: India Boosts Foreign Airline Oversight With Stricter Rules
item.description: India's Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) is overhauling rules for foreign airlines flying under bilateral agreements. The updates will require stricter oversight, bring passenger complaint systems in line with Indian standards, and use the eGCA platform for smoother application processes. More documentation and rules for urgent operational needs are also planned, signaling greater regulatory control and transparency for international airlines in India's fast-growing aviation market.

item.title: Aster DM Healthcare Creditors Approve Quality Care India Merger
item.description: Aster DM Healthcare Limited has secured strong approval from its unsecured trade creditors for the proposed merger with Quality Care India Limited. The vote, with over 80% of debt value in favour, is a crucial step toward finalising the merger, pending National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) sanction.

item.title: Maruti Interior Products Launches ₹45.3 Cr Rights Issue March 18
item.description: Maruti Interior Products Limited is launching a ₹45.30 crore rights issue on March 18, 2026. The company will offer 4.53 crore equity shares at ₹10 each, closing on March 24. This move aims to strengthen its capital structure and follows recent acquisition approvals and strong H1 FY25 financials, but it means considerable equity dilution for existing shareholders.

item.title: PI Industries to Host Investor Meetings in Mumbai; No Price-Sensitive Data to Be Shared
item.description: PI Industries Limited has scheduled analyst and investor meetings in Mumbai for March 16 and 17, 2026. The company stated it will not share any unpublished price-sensitive information during these sessions. The meetings aim to provide a platform for management to discuss the company's strategy and market position with the financial community.

item.title: Platinum Industries Schedules March 14 Investor Meeting with 11H Advisors
item.description: Platinum Industries Limited announced a virtual one-on-one investor meeting scheduled for March 14, 2026, with 11H Advisors. The discussion will be strictly limited to publicly available information, with no Un-Published Sensitive Information (UPSI) to be shared, aiming to enhance transparency.

item.title: InfoBeans Share Gift: Promoter's Future Date Sparks Questions
item.description: InfoBeans Technologies promoter Siddharth Sethi gifted 5,000 shares, slightly lowering his direct stake to 24.46%. The filing, however, listed the transfer date as March 9, 2026 – a date in the future. This unusual detail has led to immediate questions about the transaction's timing and legitimacy.

item.title: Shriram Finance to Detail Strategy at Virtual Investor Meet March 16, 2026
item.description: Shriram Finance will host a virtual investor and analyst conference on March 16, 2026, as part of the Morgan Stanley Virtual India Financials Seminar. Senior management will discuss the company's latest investor presentation and publicly available information with funds, institutional investors, and brokers.

item.title: EMS Ltd Promoter Boosts Share Pledge to 25.51% for Financing
item.description: EMS Ltd promoter Ramveer Singh pledged 21.07 lakh shares, or 3.79% of the company, to CSL Finance. This adds collateral for existing loans, supports liquidity, and meets margin calls. Total promoter shares now pledged stand at 25.51%.

item.title: Popular Vehicles Renews ₹43.6 Crore Guarantees for Subsidiaries
item.description: Popular Vehicles and Services Ltd. is continuing its financial support for its wholly-owned subsidiaries. The company renewed corporate guarantees worth ₹43.60 crore for their credit lines. This includes ₹21.60 crore for Popular Mega Motors (India) Private Limited via HDFC Bank and ₹22.00 crore for Popular Auto Dealers Private Limited via South Indian Bank, ensuring their ongoing access to banking finance. The announcement was made on March 11, 2026.

item.title: Artefact Projects Bags ₹3.28 Cr Rajasthan Highway Project
item.description: Artefact Projects Limited has secured a ₹3.28 crore consultancy contract for highway upgradation in Rajasthan. The 36-month project will cover a 162.46 km National Highway section under the Bharatmala Pariyojana, highlighting the company's contribution to India's road infrastructure.

item.title: Digitide Solutions Expands to Dubai for Middle East IT Services Growth
item.description: Digitide Solutions Limited has launched a wholly-owned subsidiary, Digitide IT Solutions L.L.C S.O.C, in Dubai, UAE, effective March 11, 2026. This move, backed by an authorized capital of AED 300,000, aims to strengthen Digitide's position in the Middle East IT services market, offering key services such as cloud, data center, and application development as part of its core strategy.

item.title: Jubilant FoodWorks Opens Raigad Supply Hub to Fuel QSR Growth
item.description: Jubilant FoodWorks Limited began operations at its new supply chain centre in Raigad, Maharashtra, on March 11, 2026. This expansion strengthens infrastructure for brands like Domino's and Popeyes, aiming to boost efficiency and support future growth in India's competitive QSR market.

item.title: Castrol India's Record Dividend Contrasts With Falling Stock Price
item.description: Castrol India will announce a record final dividend of ₹5.3 per share on March 23, 2026. This payout, the highest in a decade, offers a dividend yield of about 2.85% at the current ₹186 stock price. This contrasts sharply with the stock's year-to-date decline of -2.62%. The company's high 121% dividend payout ratio signals a focus on shareholder returns over retained earnings for growth.

item.title: Asha Devi Goenka Sells Kamadgiri Fashion Shares, Stake Drops Below 1%
item.description: Asha Devi Goenka, a promoter of Kamadgiri Fashion Limited, has offloaded 60,294 shares, trimming her stake to 0.92% from 1.95%. The transaction occurred on March 10, 2026. This move comes amidst a backdrop of challenging financial performance for the textile manufacturer, which has seen revenue and profit declines in recent quarters and faced downgrades from analysts.

item.title: Promoter Jagruti Synthetics Lifts Kamadgiri Fashion Stake to 6.40%
item.description: Kamadgiri Fashion announced Jagruti Synthetics Pvt. Ltd. acquired 60,294 shares, raising its total stake to 6.40%. This move by a promoter entity alters the company's shareholding. Kamadgiri Fashion operates in fabric and apparel manufacturing.

item.title: Snowman Logistics Rating Stable as Debt Falls; India Ratings Affirms
item.description: India Ratings affirmed Snowman Logistics' credit rating at IND A+/Stable and IND A1. The company also reduced its rated bank loans to INR 1,665 million from INR 2,025 million. A stable outlook signals ongoing financial stability for its debt.

item.title: Orient Bell Faces Production Hit as GAIL Gas Restricts Supply
item.description: Orient Bell's Hoskote plant faces an operational challenge after its gas supplier, GAIL Gas, declared a Force Majeure event. The restriction limits gas supply to 80% of the past six-month average, impacting production. The company is using existing inventory to maintain dispatches and closely monitoring the situation.

item.title: Apis India Adds 84 MT Masala Dates Capacity for ₹1.5 Cr
item.description: Apis India Limited has completed the addition of production capacity for its new Masala Dates product, increasing output by approximately 84 Metric Ton (MT) per month. The expansion, costing around ₹1.50 Crore, was financed through internal accruals. This strategic move aims to meet the production requirements for the recently launched Masala Dates range, reinforcing the company's foray into value-added snacking segments.

item.title: LTIMindtree Named GenAI Innovator by Avasant, Faces Execution Hurdles
item.description: LTIMindtree has been named an Innovator in Avasant's Generative AI Services 2025 RadarView report, earning praise for its GenAI and agentic AI capabilities. Its BlueVerse™ ecosystem is a key differentiator for scaling AI solutions, alongside a strong focus on AI governance. Shareholders await approval for the proposed name change to LTM Limited.

item.title: Aether Industries Meets India Capital Growth Fund; Shares Public Data Only
item.description: Aether Industries Limited held a physical meeting, including a site visit, with India Capital Growth Fund on March 11, 2026. All discussions were strictly limited to publicly available information, preventing the disclosure of any Unpublished Price Sensitive Information (UPSI). This meeting highlights the company's focus on transparent investor relations.

item.title: Vardhman Textiles Ventures into Power Generation After ₹168.5 Cr Q3 Profit
item.description: Vardhman Textiles Limited has secured member approval to enter the electricity generation sector. The diversification allows the textile major to produce power from conventional and renewable sources for captive use and external sales, signaling a strategic move beyond its core business amidst ongoing margin pressures.

item.title: Bombay HC Strikes Down Unlawful GST Cash Seizures
item.description: The Bombay High Court has ruled unlawful the seizure of ₹1 crore cash by the Directorate General of GST Intelligence (DGGI). The court stated GST law does not give officers power to seize cash simply because it is found during a search. This judgment, dated March 10, 2026, stresses following legal rules strictly, which will significantly affect how GST authorities conduct searches and seizures nationwide. The court called the seizure "perverse, arbitrary and without authority of law" and ordered the money returned.

item.title: Birla Holdings Sells ₹1,831 Crore Stake in Aditya Birla Lifestyle Brands
item.description: Birla Group Holdings Private Limited has sold a 2.39% stake in Aditya Birla Lifestyle Brands (ABLBL) for ₹1,831 crore via open market sales between March 2-9, 2026. The stake reduction brings the holding company's ownership down to 15.00% from 17.39%, signaling a portfolio rebalancing within the group's retail segment.

item.title: Aditya Birla Capital to Host Morgan Stanley on March 16, 2026
item.description: Aditya Birla Capital Limited announced it will hold a virtual investor meeting with Morgan Stanley on March 16, 2026. The session is part of Morgan Stanley's India Financials Tour. The company noted the schedule may change due to unforeseen circumstances from either party.
